title: After-Action Report: V2.5 — HC Ambition System, Coaching Trees, Assistant HC Meter, and Coach Development
scope: Twelve commits spanning fly.io deployment fixes, game engine balancing (touch distribution, hero ball nerfs), the coaching portal NRMP matching system, in-game dev aura, and — in the final commit — the HC ambition/departure overhaul, coaching tree tracker, assistant HC readiness meter, coach attribute development, and role fluidity between HC and coordinator positions.

rationale.item: The coaching system through V2.4 treated head coaches like permanent fixtures. An HC stayed at a school until fired or until their contract expired (50% coin flip to leave). There was no model for why a successful coach would leave a low-prestige program, no mechanism for assistant coaches to earn their way into HC candidacy beyond a simple wants_hc boolean, and no career lineage connecting a coordinator's development to the head coaches they served under.
HC departure was random, not meritocratic — A 50% coin flip decided whether an expired-contract HC left. Real coaching markets are driven by ambition: successful coaches seek bigger stages.
No parlay mechanism — Coaches who were happy at their program had no way to extend.
Assistants couldn't earn HC candidacy — The wants_hc flag was set at generation time and never changed. There was no development pathway.
No coaching tree — Every coach existed in isolation. ... no way to display a Saban-style coaching tree on the coach card.
Coaches didn't develop — Players gained attributes every offseason through apply_offseason_development, but coaches were static.

actions.description: CoachCard data model expansion: new fields conference_titles, playoff_appearances, playoff_wins, championship_appearances, coaching_tree (List[dict]), hc_meter (0-100); all serialised in to_dict()/from_dict() and survive dynasty save/load roundtrips

HC Ambition System (compute_hc_ambition): ambition = int(win_percentage * 80) + conference_titles*5 + playoff_appearances*3 + playoff_wins*4 + championship_appearances*5 + championships*8, clamped [10, 99]

Contract extension parlay (try_hc_contract_extension): no extension if ambition > team_prestige + 10 or recent record below .500; otherwise extend 3-5 new years, salary re-calculated with a 10% loyalty bump

HC departure rewrite (coaching_portal.py :: populate_coaching_portal): head coaches never leave by coin flip — try_hc_contract_extension decides stay vs portal; coordinator departure remains probabilistic 50/50

HC readiness meter (advance_hc_meter): offseason point accumulation from tenure, star player in area, team winning record, playoffs, conference titles, HC success, and own quality; thresholds at 75 (wants_hc flips, enters HC candidate pool 30% chance if overall ≥ 65) and 90 ('hot name' — always enters pool if overall ≥ 60, +8 ranking boost)

Coach attribute development (apply_coach_development): per-offseason attribute changes scaled by season record (win% ≥ .650: 0 to +2 per attribute; ≥ .500: 0 to +1; < .500: -1 to +1); age decline -1 per attribute at 60+, -2 at 65+

Role fluidity (get_acceptable_roles): fired HCs can take coordinator roles matching their affinity; coordinators with meter ≥ 75 appear in HC vacancy pools; portal free-agent generation also uses get_acceptable_roles

Coaching tree tracker (dynasty.py :: advance_season): every offseason, for each assistant, extend or append coaching_tree entries {coach_name, coach_id, team_name, year_start, year_end}; tree persists across team changes

Portal scoring updates: _team_score_coach adds postseason pedigree (conf titles ×2, playoff wins ×1.5, championship appearances ×3, championships ×5) and hot name bonus (meter ≥ 90: +8, ≥ 75: +4); _coach_score_team adds ambition matching bonus = clamp((team_prestige - ambition) × 0.3, -15, +10)

Dynasty integration: run_offseason runs three new phases between contract-ticking and portal population — HC meter advancement, coach attribute development, and postseason stat tracking plus coaching tree updates in advance_season

risk_posture: The +10 buffer is critical. A prestige-55 school can retain an HC with ambition up to 65, which covers most "solid but not elite" coaches. But a coach who wins a championship at that school jumps to ambition ~80+ and leaves — exactly as intended.
The ambition model makes departures legible — the player can look at an HC's ambition score and their team's prestige and predict whether the coach will stay.
A coordinator on a losing team with mediocre talent and an average HC gains ~6-10 points/year — they might never hit 75, which is realistic. Not every coordinator is HC material.
This creates natural retirement pressure without forcing it.

validation_claims.check_method: Import the three modules in a Python session

validation_claims.claim: Serialization roundtrip (to_dict → from_dict) — Pass — hc_meter, coaching_tree, postseason fields survive
validation_claims.check_method: Roundtrip a CoachCard through to_dict/from_dict and compare new fields
Call compute_hc_ambition with the listed profiles

validation_claims.claim: Extension parlay: modest HC at matching prestige — Extended (3 years); dominant HC at low prestige — Rejected — enters portal
validation_claims.check_method: Call try_hc_contract_extension with the two scenarios

validation_claims.claim: HC meter progression: 5 years on playoff team — 0 → 25 → 49 → 78 → 100 (wants_hc flips at year 3)
validation_claims.check_method: Simulate 5 offseasons of advance_hc_meter under playoff-team conditions

validation_claims.claim: Coach development: winning team — +2-6 total attr points per year
validation_claims.check_method: Run apply_coach_development for a winning team and sum attribute deltas

validation_claims.claim: Role fluidity: fired HC acceptable roles — [head_coach, oc] for offensive_mind affinity
validation_claims.check_method: Call get_acceptable_roles on an offensive_mind HC card

validation_claims.claim: test_dynasty.py full integration — Pass — 5-season dynasty runs cleanly

non_actions.item: Human team coaching tree display (UI panel on the coach card page)
non_actions.reason: The coaching tree data is tracked but there's no UI panel yet to display it on the coach card page.

non_actions.item: HC meter visibility in season mode UI
non_actions.reason: The meter is computed but not surfaced in the season mode UI.

non_actions.item: Interactive extension negotiation for the human team

non_actions.reason: A fired HC currently enters the portal with no penalty.
Currently only HCs get postseason stat bonuses.

open_hooks: Dynasty mode could show a 'rising star' indicator for assistants approaching 75
Extension negotiation could become interactive: 'Coach Smith wants an extension. Offer 3 years / $X?' with a risk of losing them if you lowball
Could add a 'cooling off' mechanic where recently-fired HCs accept lower salaries
Coordinators on championship teams could get a smaller HC meter boost per playoff win
section_headers_original: After-Action Report: V2.5 — HC Ambition System, Coaching Trees, Assistant HC Meter, and Coach Development
unmapped_content:
Header metadata: Branch: claude/fix-season-mode-flyio-PH0pe

Starting State (V2.4 Baseline) table: HC departure trigger — contract expired → 50% coin flip; Contract extension — none; HC ambition model — none; wants_hc flag set at generation, never updated; HC readiness meter — none; Coaching tree — none; Coach attribute development — none; Role fluidity — permanent; Postseason stat tracking — championships only on CoachCard; Portal scoring (HC candidates) — overall rating + classification fit.

Why these fields: The ambition formula needs postseason data beyond just championships. ... playoff_wins specifically rewards depth — an HC who went to three straight semifinals accumulates 6+ playoff wins, making them extremely attractive to elite programs.

Ambition profile table: First-year HC, no record ~10; 7-5 HC, 1 conf title ~54; 9-3 HC, 2 playoff trips, 1 conf title ~68; 11-1 HC, 3 playoffs, 5 PO wins, 1 championship ~92; Dynasty HC, multiple rings 99. Design rationale: win percentage provides the base (0-80 range).

Why not just re-sign at 50%? The old model was degenerate, with two failure modes: (a) a great coach randomly leaves a great program for no reason, and (b) a great coach randomly stays at a terrible program forever.

HC meter point sources table: Tenure +2/year as coordinator; Star player in area +5 to +10; Team winning record +2 to +8; Made playoffs +3 to +5; Won conference +2 to +4; HC success +2 to +6; Own quality +1 to +3 (overall ≥ 75). Progression: playoff-team coordinator gains ~20-30 points/year, hits 75 in ~3 years and 90 in ~4. Why a meter instead of a formula? A formula would be stateless; the meter is cumulative — history matters; development doesn't reset on team change.

Coach development net effect: a winning coordinator improves ~3-6 total attribute points per year; over a 5-year stint could gain +15-30 points, jumping from 65 overall to 72-75. Creates an arc: hire a young coordinator, watch them develop into a legitimate HC candidate, then promote them or lose them to the portal.

Role fluidity table: HC (defensive_mind) → head_coach, dc; HC (offensive_mind) → head_coach, oc; HC (special_teams_guru) → head_coach, stc; HC (balanced) → head_coach, oc or dc; Coordinator (meter ≥ 75) → current_role, head_coach; Coordinator (meter < 75) → current_role only.

Coaching tree example JSON: [{"coach_name": "A. Smith", "coach_id": "coach_...", "team_name": "Xavier", "year_start": 2026, "year_end": 2029}, {"coach_name": "B. Jones", ... "year_start": 2030, "year_end": 2032}].

Before/After Summary table: HC departure 50% coin flip → ambition vs. prestige comparison; Contract extension none → automatic for content coaches (ambition ≤ prestige + 10); HC candidate pool wants_hc boolean → HC meter earned through performance; Coaching tree none → full lineage; Coach development static → 0-2 attr gains/year, age decline 60+; Role fluidity permanent → HCs ↔ coordinators based on affinity + meter; Postseason tracking championships only → conf_titles, playoff_appearances, playoff_wins, championship_appearances; Portal scoring overall + classification → + pedigree bonus, hot name bonus, ambition matching.
parser_notes: Scope mentions twelve commits including fly.io deployment fixes, engine balancing, NRMP matching and dev aura, but the Work Performed section only details the final commit's coaching systems; the earlier eleven commits are not documented here. Note: this is a second 'V2.5' AAR (dated 2026-02-23) distinct from AAR_2026-02-22_film_study_v2.5.md.
